title: Salsa Fresca Chicken Bake (High-Protein Recipe) | Eati
introduction: Salsa Fresca Chicken Bake is a fresh, flavorful high-protein dinner made by baking chicken with salsa, lime, garlic, and peppers. It is simple to cook and easy to log in Eati.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- Chicken
- Fresh salsa
- Lime
- Garlic
- Peppers
- Optional: cheese
- Spices (cumin, chili powder, or your favorites)
- Salt and pepper

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Season chicken with salt, pepper, and spices.
2. Spread salsa over or under the chicken in a baking dish.
3. Add garlic and peppers; bake until chicken is cooked through.
4. Stir or spoon juices over the chicken once or twice.
5. Finish with lime juice and optional cheese; bake until cheese is melted.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 377 kcal per serving (estimated)
Fat: 11g
Protein: 58g

Tip: adjust cheese quantity to keep fat aligned with your target.

sections.heading: Why Salsa Fresca Chicken Is an Elite Fat-Loss Recipe
sections.content:
At 377 calories with 58g of protein, this recipe has one of the best protein-to-calorie ratios of any dinner — 15g of protein per 100 calories.

Macro breakdown:
• 377 calories (low)
• 58g protein (very high)
• 11g fat (low)
• ~12g net carbs (mostly from peppers and salsa)

Why the ratio is so favorable:
• Lean chicken breast with minimal added fat.
• Fresh salsa adds huge volume and flavor with near-zero calories (15 cal per 1/4 cup).
• Vegetables (peppers, onion, garlic) bulk up the plate.
• Cheese is optional, not required.

Comparison to similar dishes:
• Chipotle Chicken Bowl (brown rice, salsa, beans): 640 cal, 32g protein.
• Qdoba Chicken Naked Burrito: 700+ cal, 35g protein.
• Homemade salsa chicken bake: 377 cal, 58g protein.

That's almost double the protein for 60% of the calories. Use a protein calculator to see how much protein you actually need.

Why salsa fresca (pico de gallo) is the star:
Fresh salsa is one of the best fat-loss 'condiments' in existence — essentially raw tomato, onion, cilantro, jalapeño, and lime. You get enormous volume and flavor for almost no calories. Compare to 2 tbsp of sour cream (60 cal) or ranch dressing (140 cal).

sections.heading: Salsa Fresca vs Jarred Salsa: Big Flavor Differences
sections.content:
This recipe is called 'salsa fresca' for a reason — fresh makes a huge difference.

Fresh salsa fresca (pico de gallo, 1/4 cup):
• 15 calories
• 3g carbs, 1g fiber
• Bright, crisp, high vitamin C
• Must be used within 3 days

Jarred salsa (1/4 cup):
• 20 calories
• 4g carbs
• Cooked-down, softer flavor
• Higher sodium (varies widely — check label)
• Lasts weeks in fridge

Restaurant-style jarred (1/4 cup):
• Similar calories to homemade
• Pureed smooth texture
• Mellow flavor, less heat

Flavor differences when baked:
• Fresh salsa provides bright, crisp accents even after baking.
• Jarred salsa melts into a uniform sauce — less texture, more comforting.
• Fresh is best for 'bright and summery' vibe; jarred is better for 'comfort casserole' vibe.

5-minute homemade salsa fresca:
• 2 cups diced Roma tomatoes (cores removed)
• 1/2 cup diced white onion
• 1 jalapeño, seeded and minced
• 1/4 cup chopped cilantro
• 2 tbsp lime juice
• 1/2 tsp salt

Combine, rest 15 minutes to blend flavors. Keeps 3 days.

Pro tip: For this baked recipe, use fresh salsa on top of the chicken (added in last 5 minutes of cooking) and a layer of jarred underneath. Best of both worlds.

sections.heading: Ingredient Upgrades and Variations
sections.content:
The basic salsa chicken bake has endless variations. Here are flavor directions:

Mexican-inspired variations:
• Classic: Salsa + cumin + chili powder + lime + cheese.
• Verde version: Use salsa verde (green) instead of fresca. Milder, tangier.
• Chipotle-chocolate: Add 1 tsp cocoa powder and 1 tsp chipotle in adobo — hints of mole.
• Smoky: Add 1 tsp smoked paprika and replace some salsa with fire-roasted tomatoes.

International twists:
• Mediterranean: Swap salsa for diced tomatoes + olives + feta + oregano.
• Italian: Marinara + basil + mozzarella (essentially chicken parmesan).
• Indian: Substitute tikka masala sauce + yogurt + garam masala.
• Greek: Greek salsa (tomato, cucumber, feta, olives, oregano) + lemon.

Protein swaps:
• Chicken thighs (darker meat, +60 cal): Juicier and more forgiving.
• Turkey breast: Nearly identical macros, milder flavor.
• Shrimp (large): Cooks in 6–8 min, adjust timing.
• Ground turkey (formed into patties): Quick weeknight option.

Vegetable expansions:
• Black beans: +75 cal per 1/4 cup, adds fiber.
• Corn: +40 cal per 1/4 cup, adds sweetness.
• Zucchini or squash: Nearly free calories, adds volume.
• Roasted sweet potato: +50 cal per 1/4 cup, great for performance meals.

Cheese options:
• No cheese: 377 cal baseline.
• 1/4 oz cotija crumbled: +25 cal.
• 1/4 cup Monterey Jack melted: +110 cal.
• 1/4 cup part-skim mozzarella: +80 cal.
• Optional Greek yogurt topping (2 tbsp): +20 cal, +3g protein.

For more Mexican-inspired fat-loss recipes, see [fajita chicken casserole](/blog/fajita-chicken-casserole-recipe) and [salsa verde chicken](/blog/salsa-verde-chicken-recipe).

sections.heading: What to Serve With Salsa Fresca Chicken Bake
sections.content:
The lightness of this dish allows flexibility with sides.

Low-calorie pairings:
• Cauliflower rice (1 cup, 35 cal)
• Mixed greens salad with lime vinaigrette (60 cal)
• Roasted zucchini or peppers (50 cal per cup)
• Cabbage slaw (60 cal per cup)

Carb-inclusive pairings:
• 1/2 cup cilantro-lime rice (110 cal)
• 1/2 cup black beans (110 cal, 7g protein, 7g fiber)
• 1/2 cup quinoa (110 cal, 4g protein)
• Corn tortillas, 2 small (100 cal)

Complete plate ideas:

Fat loss (480 cal, 62g protein):
• Salsa fresca chicken bake (377)
• Cauliflower rice (35)
• 1 cup green salad w/ lime (65)

Maintenance (620 cal, 67g protein):
• Salsa fresca chicken bake (377)
• 1/2 cup cilantro-lime rice (110)
• 1/2 cup black beans (110)
• Sliced avocado (80) — optional

Performance (800 cal, 75g protein):
• Salsa fresca chicken bake w/ cheese (480)
• 2 corn tortillas (100)
• 1/2 cup rice (110)
• 1/2 cup beans (110)

Toppings bar for families:
Set out options and let people customize: Greek yogurt, avocado, fresh salsa, cilantro, lime wedges, hot sauce, shredded cabbage, cheese. Everyone gets what they want at the macros they need.

sections.heading: Meal Prep, Freezing & Leftover Magic
sections.content:
Salsa chicken bake is a meal-prep superstar.

Batch cooking:
• Double the recipe in a 9×13 dish.
• Cook time: 30–35 min instead of 25.
• Makes 6–8 servings.

Freezing:
• Chicken + salsa freezes beautifully (3 months).
• Add cheese only before reheating for best texture.
• Freeze portions in flat bags for faster thaw.

Reheating:
• Microwave: 90 seconds covered.
• Stovetop: Shred chicken, warm in small skillet 5 min.
• Oven: 350°F for 10 min covered.

7 leftover transformations:
1. Chicken tacos — shred and stuff into warm tortillas.
2. Burrito bowls — over rice with beans, salsa, avocado.
3. Nachos — over tortilla chips with cheese and jalapeños.
4. Stuffed peppers — mix with rice and stuff into bell peppers, bake 15 min.
5. Quesadillas — with extra cheese in a tortilla.
6. Chicken salad — shred cold onto greens with avocado.
7. Enchiladas — roll into tortillas, top with more salsa and cheese, bake.

Meal prep strategy:
Cook Sunday, eat 4 different ways through the week. Batch cook rice and beans separately for mix-and-match bowls. This recipe's neutral-Mexican flavor works with everything. For tracking accuracy across variations, see [how to track calories correctly](/blog/how-to-track-calories-correctly) and [best foods for weight loss](/blog/best-foods-for-weight-loss).

conclusion: This bake keeps dinner bright and satisfying while delivering strong protein. Use Eati to verify your servings match your goals.
metaTitle: Salsa Fresca Chicken Bake: 58g Protein in 377 Calories (Mexican-Inspired)
metaDescription: High-protein salsa fresca chicken bake delivers 58g protein in 377 calories per serving. Includes homemade salsa recipe, variations, side pairings, and 7 leftover ideas.
targetKeyword: salsa fresca chicken bake high protein

faqs.question: How many calories in salsa fresca chicken bake?
faqs.answer: Approximately 377 calories per serving with 58g of protein and 11g of fat. Adding 1/4 cup Monterey Jack cheese increases it to ~487 calories. Using chicken thighs instead of breast adds about 60 calories per serving.

faqs.question: Can I use jarred salsa instead of fresh?
faqs.answer: Yes, with slightly different results. Jarred salsa melts into a sauce, creating a more casserole-like dish. Fresh salsa fresca stays chunky and bright. For best of both, use jarred salsa as the base and fresh salsa as a topping added in the last 5 minutes of baking.

faqs.question: Is this recipe gluten-free?
faqs.answer: Yes, as long as your salsa is certified gluten-free (most are, but always check labels). All base ingredients — chicken, fresh salsa, peppers, garlic, lime, spices — are naturally gluten-free.

faqs.question: Can I make this in the slow cooker?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Place chicken and salsa in slow cooker, cook on low 6 hours or high 3 hours. Shred chicken, stir, and serve. Add cheese in the last 10 minutes if using. Works great for meal prep with pulled-style chicken.

faqs.question: What's the best protein-to-calorie ratio recipe?
faqs.answer: This salsa chicken bake has one of the best ratios at 15g protein per 100 calories. Other elite options include plain chicken breast (21g per 100 cal), nonfat Greek yogurt (17g per 100 cal), and white fish (18g per 100 cal). See our [high-protein low-calorie foods](/blog/high-protein-low-calorie-foods) guide for more.
